content.content:
1	# XAGENT
2	
3	Runs coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) inside remote sandboxes.
4	
5	## Features
6	
7	- **Self-hosted runners** - Run agents on your own infrastructure inside Docker containers
8	- **Third-party integrations** - Interact with agents via GitHub and Jira events
9	- **MCP server** - Create and manage tasks from Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client
10	
11	## Web UI
12	
13	![Web UI](images/webui.png)
14	
15	## Quick Start
16	
17	Install `xagent` cli:
18	
19	```bash
20	mise run install
21	```
22	
23	Download the pre-built binaries (if needed):
24	
25	```bash
26	xagent download
27	```
28	
29	Create an API key in the Web UI (https://xagent.choly.ca/ui/keys/new) and copy the returned `xat_…` value into `~/.config/xagent/config.yaml`:
30	
31	```yaml
32	token: xat_...
33	```
34	
35	Create a `workspaces.yaml` file (see examples below):
36	
37	```bash
38	vim ~/.config/xagent/workspaces.yaml
39	```
40	
41	Start the local runner:
42	
43	```bash
44	xagent runner
45	```
46	
47	Create and monitor tasks via the Web UI.
48	
49	Open: https://xagent.choly.ca/
50	
51	## Workspace Examples
52	
53	See [examples/workspaces/](examples/workspaces/) for workspace configuration examples:
54	
55	- [claude.yml](examples/workspaces/claude.yml) - Claude Code
56	- [codex.yml](examples/workspaces/codex.yml) - OpenAI Codex
57	- [cursor.yml](examples/workspaces/cursor.yml) - Cursor Agent
58	- [copilot.yml](examples/workspaces/copilot.yml) - GitHub Copilot
59	- [mcp-server.yml](examples/workspaces/mcp-server.yml) - MCP server configuration
60	- [private-repo.yml](examples/workspaces/private-repo.yml) - Cloning private repositories
61	- [dummy.yml](examples/workspaces/dummy.yml) - Dummy agent for testing
62	
63	## Docker Compose Runner
64	
65	See [examples/runner/](examples/runner/) for running the runner as a Docker Compose service with a pull-through registry cache.
66	
67	## Debugging
68	
69	View container logs:
70	
71	```bash
72	xagent logs -f <taskid>
73	```
74	
75	Set `verbose: true` on a workspace's agent to bypass the CLI output parser and
76	log every raw line. Useful when the parser is hiding details (errors,
77	intermediate output, tool-use payloads) you want to inspect via `xagent logs`.
78	
79	```yaml
80	agent:
81	  type: claude
82	  verbose: true
83	```
84	
85	Get a shell to a task container:
86	
87	```bash
88	xagent shell <taskid>
89	```
90	
91	List task containers:
92	
93	```bash
94	xagent containers
95	```
96	
97	## Local Development
98	
99	```bash
100	# Start server and postgres locally
101	docker compose up -d
102	
103	# Run the FE
104	cd webui
105	pnpm install
106	pnpm run dev
107	```
108	
109	The local server runs with `--no-auth`, but the runner still requires an API key.
110	Create one in the local Web UI at http://localhost:5173/ui/keys/new, then start the runner:
111	
112	```bash
113	xagent runner --server http://localhost:6464 -key <api-key>
114	```
115	
116	
117	### Build
118	
119	```bash
120	mise run build      # Build main + prebuilt binaries (linux amd64/arm64)
121	mise run generate   # Generate protobuf code
122	go build            # Build main binary only
123	```
124	
125	## Events
126	
127	Agents can attach **links** to their tasks for external resources they create (PRs, Jira issues, etc.). Links created with `subscribe=true` act as subscriptions. When a new event occurs on the resource, the agent is automatically restarted to respond.
128	
129	![Events](images/events.svg)
130	
131	## Architecture
132	
133	![Architecture](images/architecture.svg)
134	
135	
136	## Schema
137	
138	![Schema](images/schema.svg)
139

file.content:
# XAGENT

Runs coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) inside remote sandboxes.

## Features

- **Self-hosted runners** - Run agents on your own infrastructure inside Docker containers
- **Third-party integrations** - Interact with agents via GitHub and Jira events
- **MCP server** - Create and manage tasks from Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client

## Web UI

![Web UI](images/webui.png)

## Quick Start

Install `xagent` cli:

```bash
mise run install
```

Download the pre-built binaries (if needed):

```bash
xagent download
```

Create an API key in the Web UI (https://xagent.choly.ca/ui/keys/new) and copy the returned `xat_…` value into `~/.config/xagent/config.yaml`:

```yaml
token: xat_...
```

Create a `workspaces.yaml` file (see examples below):

```bash
vim ~/.config/xagent/workspaces.yaml
```

Start the local runner:

```bash
xagent runner
```

Create and monitor tasks via the Web UI.

Open: https://xagent.choly.ca/

## Workspace Examples

See [examples/workspaces/](examples/workspaces/) for workspace configuration examples:

- [claude.yml](examples/workspaces/claude.yml) - Claude Code
- [codex.yml](examples/workspaces/codex.yml) - OpenAI Codex
- [cursor.yml](examples/workspaces/cursor.yml) - Cursor Agent
- [copilot.yml](examples/workspaces/copilot.yml) - GitHub Copilot
- [mcp-server.yml](examples/workspaces/mcp-server.yml) - MCP server configuration
- [private-repo.yml](examples/workspaces/private-repo.yml) - Cloning private repositories
- [dummy.yml](examples/workspaces/dummy.yml) - Dummy agent for testing

## Docker Compose Runner

See [examples/runner/](examples/runner/) for running the runner as a Docker Compose service with a pull-through registry cache.

## Debugging

View container logs:

```bash
xagent logs -f <taskid>
```

Set `verbose: true` on a workspace's agent to bypass the CLI output parser and
log every raw line. Useful when the parser is hiding details (errors,
intermediate output, tool-use payloads) you want to inspect via `xagent logs`.

```yaml
agent:
  type: claude
  verbose: true
```

Get a shell to a task container:

```bash
xagent shell <taskid>
```

List task containers:

```bash
xagent containers
```

## Local Development

```bash
# Start server and postgres locally
docker compose up -d

# Run the FE
cd webui
pnpm install
pnpm run dev
```

The local server runs with `--no-auth`, but the runner still requires an API key.
Create one in the local Web UI at http://localhost:5173/ui/keys/new, then start the runner:

```bash
xagent runner --server http://localhost:6464 -key <api-key>
```


### Build

```bash
mise run build      # Build main + prebuilt binaries (linux amd64/arm64)
mise run generate   # Generate protobuf code
go build            # Build main binary only
```

## Events

Agents can attach **links** to their tasks for external resources they create (PRs, Jira issues, etc.). Links created with `subscribe=true` act as subscriptions. When a new event occurs on the resource, the agent is automatically restarted to respond.

![Events](images/events.svg)

## Architecture

![Architecture](images/architecture.svg)


## Schema

![Schema](images/schema.svg)

content.text:
Done. Read-only exploration, no changes made.

## Top-level files

The repo root contains config and tooling files plus these key directories:

- **Dirs**: `cmd/`, `internal/`, `webui/`, `proto/`, `proposals/`, `examples/`, `containers/`, `n8n-node/`, `prebuilt/`, `images/`
- **Go**: `go.mod`, `go.sum`, `generate.go`
- **Build/tooling**: `mise.toml`, `buf.gen.yaml`, `buf.yaml`, `sqlc.yaml`, `.dbmate.toml`, `Dockerfile`(+`.dev`)
- **Compose**: `docker-compose.yml`, `.dev.yml`, `.test.yml`
- **Deploy/CI**: `fly.toml`, `.github/`, `.conform.yaml`, `.release-please-config.json`, `renovate.json`
- **Docs/secrets**: `README.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `sops.env.yml`, `github-app-manifest.json`
- A built `xagent` binary (~47MB) and `claude-session.jsonl` are also present.

## README.md

XAGENT runs coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) inside remote Docker sandboxes. Highlights: self-hosted runners, GitHub/Jira event integrations, and an MCP server for task management. Covers quick start (install via `mise`, API key config, `workspaces.yaml`, `xagent runner`), workspace examples, debugging (`xagent logs`/`shell`/`containers`, `verbose: true`), local dev, and an events/links subscription model.

## go.mod

- **Module**: `github.com/icholy/xagent`, **Go 1.26.0**
- **Direct deps** of note: `connectrpc.com/connect` (Connect RPC), `jackc/pgx/v5` (Postgres), `modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk` (MCP), `docker/docker` + `docker/cli`, `urfave/cli/v3`, `google/go-github/v88`, `zitadel/oidc` + `zitadel-go` (auth), OpenTelemetry stack, `yaml.v3`, `gotest.tools/v3`.
- **Tools**: buf, moq, sqlc, stringer.

## `func main` locations

Three entry points:
- `cmd/xagent/main.go:12` — the main CLI
- `cmd/migrate/main.go:10` — DB migrations
- `cmd/dummymcp/main.go:14` — a dummy MCP server

(The fourth match was just text inside `claude-session.jsonl`, not real code.)

## `go vet ./...`

Passed cleanly — no output, no warnings or errors.
